Title: GCN5-related histone acetyltransferase HOOKLESS2 regulates fungal resistance and growth in tomato

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BTB/POZ and TAZ domain-containing protein; BTB domain scaffold/adaptor protein
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with HLS2-dependent fungal defense through HLS2-mediated activation of SlBT1 expression
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR for H3Ac at promoter; ChIP-qPCR showing HLS2-MYC association with SlBT1 promoter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to pathogen defense programs, likely linked to jasmonate-responsive immunity
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced by Botrytis cinerea in wild type leaves; induction significantly reduced in slhls2 mutants
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CUL3; RBX1; BTB-containing E3 ubiquitin ligase complex components
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Promoter H3 acetylation is reduced in slhls2 mutant; HLS2-MYC directly associates with the SlBT1 promoter
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream marker/target for improving defense-associated proteostasis pathways, but direct trait engineering value was not functionally tested here
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlBT1 is one of the most prominent HLS2-dependent induced genes after Botrytis cinerea infection. 2. SlHLS2 directly associates with the SlBT1 promoter and is required for H3 acetylation at this locus. 3. Reduced SlBT1 induction in slhls2 correlates with higher fungal susceptibility, supporting SlBT1 as a direct downstream defense-related target.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper names both SlBT1 and SlBTB1 in summary/discussion context; the validated locus in results is SlBT1, encoding a BTB/POZ and TAZ domain-containing protein likely acting as an adaptor in a CUL3-RBX1-BTB E3 ligase complex.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HLS2 is required for full Botrytis-induced expression of ASAT3, linking HLS2 to acylsugar-associated defense metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR showing lack of direct HLS2/H3Ac enrichment at ASAT3 locus
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against insect pests and likely broader biotic defense
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Botrytis cinerea-induced in wild type; reduced induction in slhls2 mutants
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No significant HLS2-MYC enrichment or H3Ac difference at ASAT3 locus, suggesting indirect regulation by HLS2
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream defense-metabolism target for acylsugar improvement, though direct functional manipulation was not tested in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ASAT3 is among the defense/metabolism genes whose pathogen-induced expression requires SlHLS2. 2. ASAT3 induction is reduced in slhls2 mutants during Botrytis cinerea infection. 3. ChIP evidence indicates this regulation is indirect rather than through direct HLS2 binding to the ASAT3 locus.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ASAT3 was treated as a core downstream gene because it is specifically validated by qPCR and ChIP follow-up in Results, and directly links the study to specialized defense metabolite biosynthesis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HLS2 is required for full pathogen-induced ATG11 expression, linking HLS2 to autophagy-associated metabolic remobilization and defense
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports autophagy-associated nutrient remobilization and stress adaptation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Botrytis cinerea in wild type; reduced induction in slhls2 mutants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible marker for HLS2-dependent defense/autophagy competence, but direct engineering value not established
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATG11 is a validated downstream gene whose Botrytis-induced expression depends on SlHLS2. 2. Compromised ATG11 induction in slhls2 mutants supports a role for HLS2 in autophagy-linked defense responses.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because specifically validated in Results as a prominent HLS2-dependent gene tied to autophagy and senescence-related metabolism.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: HLS2 is required for full pathogen-induced expression of an ABA catabolic enzyme gene
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormone homeostasis influencing defense and stress responses
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Botrytis cinerea-induced in wild type; significantly lower in hls2 mutants after inoculation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node connecting chromatin regulation and hormone catabolism during defense, but not directly functionally tested here
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP707A (Solyc01g108210), encoding an ABA 8'-hydroxylase, is induced by Botrytis cinerea in wild type plants. 2. This induction is significantly reduced in slhls2 mutants, linking SlHLS2 to ABA catabolic gene regulation during defense.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although an enzyme gene, it is treated here as a validated downstream common gene rather than core pathway gene because the paper does not functionally interrogate CYP707A itself as a primary experimental subject.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GCN5-like histone acetyltransferase; acetyltransferase domain PF00583
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Histone H3 acetylation-associated transcriptional activation; direct chromatin association; broader positive regulation of pathogen-induced genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Defense-related specialized metabolism and signaling, especially jasmonate/oxylipin biosynthesis-associated transcription; broader control of acylsugar-associated defense and autophagy-linked responses
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlBT1; jasmonate biosynthesis genes; ATG11; ASAT3; IST1; SAC8; GLIP1; CYP707A; kinesin; AP2/ERF network genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates or is required for full pathogen-induced expression of SlBT1 and multiple defense/metabolism genes; loss of SlHLS2 compromises induction of jasmonate biosynthesis, oxylipin, autophagy, ubiquitination, and acylsugar-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes fungal resistance and proper growth; required for B. cinerea-induced defense gene expression and indirectly supports jasmonate/oxylipin and acylsugar-associated responses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major chromatin-level positive regulator required for pathogen-induced defense metabolic programs, including jasmonate biosynthesis-associated responses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout mutants; overexpression (SlHLS2-myc) lines; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR for H3Ac and HLS2-MYC promoter association; pathogen assays; ROS assays; subcellular localization; immunoblot with MG132
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against necrotrophic fungal pathogens and insect-associated biotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all tested tomato tissues, highest in petals, then sepals; induced by Botrytis cinerea, ABA, IAA, and ACC; reduced by SA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed broadly; strongly induced in infected leaves after B. cinerea inoculation and by ABA, IAA, ACC
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Botrytis cinerea infection; chitin-related immune signaling; ABA; IAA; ACC; SA negatively affects expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely functions within chromatin regulatory/coactivator complexes; no tomato complex members directly validated in this study
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct epigenetic regulator itself; Slhls2 mutation reduces histone H3 acetylation at the SlBT1 promoter; HLS2-MYC associates with SlBT1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Botrytis cinerea inoculation; Alternaria solani infection; chitin; flg22; dark-induced senescence; ABA; IAA; ACC; SA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severe dwarf/bushy architecture, delayed germination and growth, infertility/parthenocarpy, altered leaf morphology, altered auxin accumulation; broad pleiotropy suggests engineering risk
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve fungal resistance, but deployment requires caution because strong perturbation causes major developmental and fertility penalties; fine-tuning or tissue/inducible strategies may be preferable
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlHLS2 encodes a GCN5-related histone acetyltransferase induced by pathogens and several hormones. 2. CRISPR slhls2 mutants are more susceptible to Botrytis cinerea and Alternaria solani, whereas SlHLS2 overexpression enhances Botrytis resistance. 3. SlHLS2 is required for full induction of defense-associated genes, including jasmonate biosynthesis-related, autophagy-related, ubiquitination-related, and acylsugar-associated genes. 4. SlHLS2 directly associates with the SlBT1 promoter and is required for histone H3 acetylation at this locus. 5. SlHLS2 protein undergoes proteasome-sensitive degradation after chitin treatment.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although the paper concerns nutrient/metabolism-related regulation, SlHLS2 is not a classic metabolic enzyme or TF but a chromatin regulator with broad developmental and immune effects; direct evidence is strongest for SlBT1 regulation, whereas many metabolic outputs are indirect.
